content: Just as the pedagogical use of written reflexion in high English curricula trains students to re-examine their own written arguments against alternative interpretations to improve critical thinking, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to adopt the structured 'writing workshop reflexion' framework rather than relying solely on inconsistent ad-hoc internal perspective checks. This structured, iterative peer and self-review workflow from literacy education addresses the core flaw of current LLM self-reflection that struggles to generate consistent, meaningful critique of its own outputs.
